title: support for storage for rootless containers

comments.body: tests are probably failing for https://github.com/opencontainers/runc/pull/1816
@rhatdan the patch for runc got merged (b222ea4469dd5e304f3f6cf7a2482860285639a2), could we get an updated build?
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably 4b4de5d) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably 16ea659) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
This is currently ready for review.  The updated runc is used only on Travis right now (and tests are happy there).  Everywhere else, we need a newer runc

LGTM
@mheon @nalind @mtrmac @baude @umohnani8 @wking PTAL

> The updated runc is used only on Travis right now (and tests are happy there). Everywhere else, we need a newer runc

Having different runc versions in different places makes me jumpy.  Is there something that's blocking us bumping to a single new version of runc in all of our locations (`Dockerfile`, `vendor.conf`, other places?)?

The global user namespace approach you have here is just mapping a single user and group to 0 (e.g. you're not using [`subuid(5)`][1] to map multiple UIDs).  Is that really sufficient for unpacking images?  I'd expect at least some images to require more than one user or group ID.  A more robust fix might be possible if we handled this with [shiftfs][2], or if containers/storage grew an API for specifying ID translations (like GNU tar's [`--owner-map`][3], etc.), or if it could use something like [umoci's unpriv package][4].  And regardless of how we handle ID mapping, there's probably no way an unprivileged user will be able to usefully unpack an image that contains device nodes, etc.

[1]: http://man7.org/linux/man-pages/man5/subuid.5.html
[2]: https://lkml.org/lkml/2018/6/15/585
[3]: https://www.gnu.org/software/tar/manual/html_node/override.html#SEC69
[4]: https://godoc.org/github.com/openSUSE/umoci/pkg/unpriv
I've kept the implementation in a new pkg so that it can be more easily re-used. @nalind could this be useful for Buildah?
bot, retest this please
I'll have to drop "Dockerfile: update runc" once the same patch from @rhatdan is merged
unfortunately tests won't pass until the newer runc is available (or I will have to disable these tests when the version is outdated)
the new runc makes the tests happy! :tada:  :tada:  :tada:

LGTM
@mheon @wking @baude @umohnani8 @TomSweeneyRedHat PTAL
LGTM, assuming happy tests
@mheon PTAL and then we can merge, if this is ok with you.

I'd like to see a bit more documentation about how rootless works, particularly how it makes a separate set of containers that you can't see with UID 0 podman (separate DB and store). Code LGTM though.

> I'd like to see a bit more documentation about how rootless works, particularly how it makes a separate set of containers that you can't see with UID 0 podman (separate DB and store). Code LGTM though.

I've added a patch to explain that rootless mode is supported and how these containers are managed
@giuseppe is there a way to specify the storage to point to something other than XDG_DATA_DIR or $HOME/.containers/storage?
it is still possible to override the default with `--root`
thanks. @giusepp.  is that for a particular image rootfs? Or does this configure a basedir in which all pulled images end up in?

that is for the single usage of podman.  Every process can use a different image store.  If you want it to be persistent you can change its value `/etc/containers/storage.conf`.  rootless mode doesn't honor this global file, we will need to teach podman how to read a per user configuration file (under $HOME/.config).
thanks @giuseppe  -- yes I think a user-defined config file, in which one can set a user-scope storegae location would be super useful.
@lukasheinrich: added a patch to read a configuration file for the rootless case

~~~hm please hold the merge, I've just noticed an issue with "run --rm"~~~

Fixed separately in #1001 and #1002
Yes lets keep them separate directories.
I addressed all the comments above, is there anything more blocking it?
Now that #1001 is in, I have no more objections. LGTM.
